description: Change ready_issues() in db.rs to remove the hardcoded bug exclusion. Replace 'issue_type IN (task, test, chore)' with a condition that includes bugs only when they have zero non-closed fixes children: (issue_type \!= 'bug' OR id NOT IN (SELECT fixes FROM issues WHERE fixes IS NOT NULL AND status \!= 'closed')). title: Update auto-close to all-or-nothing for multi-fix bugs
description: Change close_issue() in db.rs to check whether ALL issues with fixes pointing to the same bug are closed before auto-closing the bug. Currently (db.rs:312-327) the first fix task to close immediately closes the bug. New behavior: query 'SELECT COUNT(*) FROM issues WHERE fixes = ?bug_id AND status \!= closed AND id \!= ?closing_task_id'. If count > 0, skip auto-close. If 0, auto-close with reason 'fixed' (not 'fixed by <task-id>' since multiple tasks may contribute). See specs/pensa.md fixes field description. Source: crates/pensa/src/db.rs:312-327.

title: Implement double Ctrl+C confirmation in run_afk
description: In run_afk(), change the interrupt polling loop to implement double-press logic: (1) When sigint_count == 1: print 'Press Ctrl+C again to stop' to stdout, record Instant::now() as confirmation window start. (2) When sigint_count >= 2: kill child, return. (3) On each poll iteration, if confirmation window is active and 2 seconds have elapsed: reset sigint_count to 0 via store(0, Relaxed). Message goes to stdout only (not log file). See specs/ralph.md § Double Ctrl+C in AFK Mode. Source: crates/ralph/src/main.rs lines 343-471 (run_afk function).

sgf kills ralph on first Ctrl+C in AFK mode — double-press never works. orchestrate.rs registers SIGINT with AtomicBool (single-press semantics), so sgf sends SIGTERM to ralph within 50ms of the first Ctrl+C, before the user can press again. Ralph's double-Ctrl+C mechanism is correct but never gets a chance to execute.
close_reason: fixed

title: Replace AtomicBool SIGINT handler with AtomicUsize counter in orchestrate::run_ralph for AFK mode. Use signal_hook::low_level::register (like ralph does) to increment a counter. While the ralph child is running in AFK mode: first SIGINT does nothing (let ralph handle it), second SIGINT within 2s sends SIGTERM to ralph child + stops sandbox + exits 130, timeout resets counter to 0. SIGTERM always kills immediately. Outside the child-running loop (pre-launch, daemon startup, etc.), single SIGINT exits immediately.

Sandbox 'already exists' message leaks into ralph output
description: ralph's ensure_sandbox() calls `docker sandbox create` without checking if the sandbox already exists. When it does exist, Docker prints 'sandbox with name claude-<workspace> already exists' to stdout, which leaks into ralph's output between the banner and the first iteration. Fix: extract a sandbox_name() helper (shared with configure_sandbox_network), check `docker sandbox ls -q` for the name, and only call `create` if absent. Suppress stdout/stderr on the `create` call. See specs/ralph.md 'Main Loop' and crates/ralph/src/main.rs:111-166.

title: Fix: ralph save/restore terminal settings (tcgetattr/tcsetattr) around agent spawn to prevent agent from disabling ISIG via inherited stderr fd
description: The agent (Claude Code) modifies terminal settings via inherited stderr fd (e.g., tcsetattr to enable raw mode), disabling ISIG. This causes Ctrl+C to send byte 0x03 instead of SIGINT and Ctrl+D to send 0x04 instead of EOF. Fix: save terminal settings before agent spawn, restore after exit. Affects both AFK and interactive modes. See specs/ralph.md Terminal Settings Preservation section.
close_reason: Implemented terminal settings save/restore via tcgetattr/tcsetattr around agent spawn in ralph main.rs

title: Fix: sgf gate monitor_stdin and setsid on config.afk in run_ralph
description: orchestrate.rs run_ralph() unconditionally uses setsid() and monitor_stdin: true. In non-AFK (interactive) mode this causes: (1) stdin monitor thread consumes user keystrokes meant for Claude, (2) setsid prevents child from receiving terminal signals and accessing controlling terminal. Fix: only use setsid() and monitor_stdin: true when config.afk is true. See specs/springfield.md and specs/shutdown.md.
close_reason: Gated monitor_stdin and setsid on config.afk in run() and run_ralph(). Added test for non-AFK mode.

title: AFK mode stdin isolation: agent inherits terminal fd and disables ISIG, preventing Ctrl+C/Ctrl+D from working
description: In AFK mode, sgf passes Stdio::inherit() for stdin to ralph, and ralph doesn't set stdin on the agent spawn. The agent (Claude Code) inherits the terminal fd and modifies terminal settings (disabling ISIG via tcsetattr). This causes Ctrl+C to emit byte 0x03 instead of SIGINT and Ctrl+D to emit byte 0x04 instead of EOF. Fix: pass Stdio::null() for stdin in AFK mode at both sgf→ralph and ralph→agent boundaries.
close_reason: Fixed by passing Stdio::null() for stdin in AFK mode at both sgf->ralph and ralph->agent boundaries. Updated specs/shutdown.md, specs/ralph.md, specs/springfield.md to document stdin isolation.

Includes produces/consumes for context passing between iters. Create corresponding prompt files.
close_reason: Created multi-iter spec.toml with 5 iters and corresponding prompt files. Updated test to validate multi-iter structure.

title: Update README.md with cursus documentation
description: Document cursus in README.md: what cursus pipelines are, TOML format overview, how to create single-iter and multi-iter cursus definitions, context passing, stall recovery, sgf resume for cursus runs, migration from config.toml. Update any existing command documentation to reflect cursus-based resolution.
close_reason: Documented cursus pipelines in README.md: TOML format, single/multi-iter definitions, context passing, sentinel transitions, stall recovery, command resolution, and migration from config.toml

title: Integration test: single-iter cursus execution
description: Binary-level test: create tempdir with a single-iter build.toml cursus and prompt file. Use RALPH_COMMAND mock. Verify sgf dispatches to cursus runner, iter executes, sentinel cleanup, exit 0. Equivalent behavior to current sgf build.
close_reason: Implemented 3 binary-level integration tests covering single-iter cursus dispatch, alias resolution, sentinel cleanup, and stall behavior.

title: Integration test: multi-iter cursus with transitions and context passing
description: Binary-level tests: (1) Multi-iter happy path (discuss->draft->review->approve, all complete, exit 0). (2) Reject transition (review signals .ralph-reject, jumps to draft). (3) Revise transition (review signals .ralph-revise, jumps to revise, then back to review). (4) Context passing (discuss produces summary, draft consumes it, summary content in system prompt). (5) Stall recovery (draft exhausts iterations, exit 2, metadata persisted). (6) Resume stalled run. (7) Layered resolution (local TOML overrides global).
close_reason: Added 7 multi-iter cursus integration tests covering transitions, context passing, stall recovery, resume, and layered resolution

title: clippy collapsible_if lint failure in state.rs:186
description: cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings fails on crates/springfield/src/cursus/state.rs:186 with collapsible_if lint. The nested if statement (is_stale_run + if let Some) should be collapsed into a single condition with let-chain.
close_reason: Collapsed nested if into let-chain. clippy --workspace passes clean.

title: Add shutdown signal integration tests for ralph binary
description: The shutdown spec lists 4 integration tests that should exist at both sgf and ralph level. The sgf-level tests exist in crates/springfield/tests/integration.rs but ralph has none. Add to crates/ralph/tests/integration.rs: (1) double_ctrl_c_exits_130 — send two SIGINTs to ralph, verify exit code 130, (2) single_ctrl_c_continues_after_timeout — send one SIGINT, wait 3s, verify process continues, (3) sigterm_exits_immediately — send SIGTERM, verify immediate exit with code 130, (4) confirmation_message_on_first_ctrl_c — send one SIGINT, verify stderr contains 'Press Ctrl-C again to exit'. Use RALPH_COMMAND env var to inject a mock agent script (see springfield integration tests for pattern).

title: Fix home-dir fallback to skip global lookups when unresolvable
description: Spec says 'Home directory unresolvable → Warning to stderr, skip global lookups.' Current code falls back to cwd instead of skipping. Change resolve_context_files to accept Option<PathBuf> for home, and skip global tier when None.
close_reason: Fixed home-dir fallback: resolve_context_files now accepts Option<&Path> for home, skips global lookups when None

title: Remove dead has_global flag from resolve.rs layered lookup
description: The has_global field in the layered array is always true, making the conditional on it dead logic. Simplify by removing the flag and always checking both tiers.

title: Add README.md documentation for claude-wrapper crate
description: No README.md exists for the claude-wrapper crate. Add one covering purpose, installation, usage, and design goals.

title: Add unit test for home-dir-unresolvable resolution path
description: After fixing resolve_context_files to accept Option<PathBuf> for home, add a unit test: when home is None, only local .sgf/ paths are checked and global lookups are skipped entirely.

title: Add integration test for ralph → cl end-to-end invocation
description: Create an integration test that verifies the ralph → cl pipeline: ralph spawns cl with correct arguments, cl resolves context files and forwards to the mock downstream binary. Validates the full invocation chain works end-to-end.

title: Add banner field to IterDefinition and pass --banner to ralph
description: IterDefinition in cursus/toml.rs is missing the banner field (bool, default false). Add it with #[serde(default)]. In runner.rs invoke_ralph, conditionally pass --banner when iter.banner is true. Add unit tests: parse cursus with banner field (true, false, default).
close_reason: Added banner field to IterDefinition and --banner passthrough to ralph invocation

title: Rename [[iters]] to [[iter]] per spec design decision
description: The spec requires [[iter]] (singular) for TOML array tables, with a design decision explaining why. Current code uses pub iters field which maps to [[iters]] in TOML. Add #[serde(rename = "iter")] to the iters field in CursusDefinition. Update all existing .toml cursus files (global and local) from [[iters]] to [[iter]].
close_reason: Renamed [[iters]] to [[iter]] in serde, all TOML files, and all test strings. Build, lint, and all tests pass.

title: Remove legacy config.toml fallback from command resolution
description: The spec states: 'All commands are defined by cursus TOML files. There is no fallback mechanism.' Current resolve_command in main.rs has two legacy fallback steps: (1) bare prompt file resolution and (2) config.toml alias resolution. Remove both legacy fallbacks, the ResolvedCommand::Legacy variant, run_legacy_dispatch function, and related tests. Ensure all commands have cursus TOML files before removing fallback.

title: Add banner flag integration test
description: Add integration test: create cursus TOML with banner = true on an iter. Use RALPH_COMMAND mock. Verify ralph receives --banner flag. This is the 9th spec-required integration test (currently missing).

title: Create missing global cursus TOML files
description: The spec architecture section lists 7 standard global cursus files. Currently missing: verify.toml, test.toml, test-plan.toml, issues-log.toml. Create these in ~/.sgf/cursus/ with appropriate prompts, modes, and iteration counts. Use [[iter]] (singular) per the rename task dependency.

title: Fix section move HTTP method from POST to PATCH
description: The forma spec HTTP API section specifies section move as PATCH /specs/:stem/sections/:slug/move, but daemon.rs uses post(move_section) on that route. Change the route in daemon.rs from post() to patch(), update client.rs to use PATCH instead of POST, and update any tests that hit this endpoint.
close_reason: Changed section move HTTP method from POST to PATCH in daemon.rs route, client.rs request, and integration test

title: Update forma spec architecture section to reflect actual file layout
description: The architecture section lists 5 files in src/ (main.rs, db.rs, daemon.rs, types.rs, output.rs) but the codebase has 7 files (also client.rs and lib.rs). The tests section only lists integration.rs but cli_client.rs also exists. Update the architecture section via fm section set to reflect the actual file layout.

title: Add crates/forma/README.md
description: The forma crate lacks a README.md. Add a brief README covering: what forma is, how to build/install, and a pointer to the spec (.forma/specs/forma.md) for full documentation.

title: Integration test: verify all JSON output shapes match forma spec
description: Add integration tests that verify JSON output from every fm command matches the shapes documented in the spec's JSON Output section. Key shapes to verify: spec objects (stem, src, purpose, status, created_at, updated_at), spec detail objects (+ sections, refs arrays), section objects (name, slug, kind, body, position), event objects, delete/ref add/ref remove/section remove responses, count/status responses, check/doctor/export/import responses.

title: Integration test: forma + ralph end-to-end spec validation
description: Add an integration test that verifies forma works end-to-end in a realistic workflow: create specs, fill sections, add refs, export to JSONL/markdown, delete DB, import from JSONL, verify all data round-trips correctly, run fm check and verify validation report, run fm doctor and verify health. This should exercise the full lifecycle described in the spec.

title: Add E2E test: CRUD lifecycle (create → show → update → close → reopen → close)
description: Add integration test covering the full CRUD lifecycle as specified in the testing section: create an issue, show it, update fields, close it, reopen it, close again. Verify each state transition produces correct output and status changes.

title: Add E2E test: multi-fix all-or-nothing auto-close
description: Add integration test for multi-fix all-or-nothing auto-close: create bug, create two fix tasks with --fixes, close first fix task, verify bug still open, close second fix task, verify bug auto-closed with reason 'fixed'. This scenario is specified in the testing section but missing from the test suite.

title: Add E2E test: ready type filter excludes bugs
description: Add integration test: create a bug (no fix tasks), verify it appears in pn ready, then run pn ready -t task and verify the bug is excluded. Specified in testing section but missing from test suite.

title: Add E2E test: concurrent claims (two rapid claims, exactly one succeeds)
description: Add integration test for concurrent claim semantics: create an issue, fire two rapid claim requests simultaneously, verify exactly one succeeds with the issue and the other fails with already_claimed. Tests the atomic claim guarantee under concurrency.

title: Add E2E test: claim semantics full flow
description: Add integration test for full claim semantics: create issue, claim it (verify in_progress + assignee set), attempt second claim (verify already_claimed error and reports who holds it), release (verify back to open with no assignee), claim again (verify succeeds). Covers the full claim lifecycle specified in the testing section.

title: Add E2E test: dependency flow with ready/blocked
description: Add integration test for the full dependency → ready flow: create two issues, add dep (child depends on parent), verify child excluded from pn ready, verify child appears in pn blocked, close parent, verify child now appears in pn ready. Tests dependency-aware querying end-to-end.

title: Add --banner CLI flag to make startup banner conditional
description: The spec says --banner controls whether the ASCII art startup banner is displayed (default false, controlled by cursus TOML iter banner field). Currently the banner is always printed unconditionally. Add a banner: bool field to the Cli struct with #[arg(long)], and gate the print_banner() call on this flag.
close_reason: Implemented --banner flag with tests

title: Remove --spec flag and forma spec-fetching code from ralph
description: The ralph spec CLI Interface section does not include a --spec flag. The codebase has spec-fetching code (fetch_spec_markdown, render_spec_markdown, resolve_spec_content, FmSpecDetail, FmSection, FmRefSpec structs, and spec-related arg building in build_append_system_prompt_args). Remove all spec-related code and the --spec CLI option to align with the spec. Note: springfield orchestrate.rs passes --spec to ralph — that call site must also be updated.

title: Add integration tests for conditional banner display and box format
description: Add integration tests per spec Testing section for conditional banner display: (1) AFK startup banner shown with --banner flag, (2) AFK startup banner hidden without --banner flag. NOTE: Box format tests (iteration/completion/max-iterations banner) already exist. Depends on pn-a6681a60 (--banner flag implementation).

title: Add integration tests for AFK tool result hiding and NO_COLOR support
description: Add integration tests per spec Testing section: (1) AFK hides tool results — stdout does not contain tool result content from user events, (2) ANSI output disabled with NO_COLOR=1 — stdout contains no ANSI escape sequences when NO_COLOR env var is set.
close_reason: Tests afk_hides_tool_results and afk_no_color_disables_ansi already exist in crates/ralph/tests/integration.rs

title: Add integration tests for signal handling (double Ctrl+C abort, single Ctrl+C reset)
description: Add integration tests per spec Testing section: (1) AFK double Ctrl+C aborts — send two SIGINTs in quick succession, assert exit code 130 and stdout contains 'Press Ctrl+C again to stop', (2) AFK single Ctrl+C resets after timeout — send one SIGINT, iterations continue, exit code 2.
close_reason: Tests afk_double_ctrlc_aborts and afk_single_ctrlc_resets_after_timeout already exist in crates/ralph/tests/integration.rs

title: Add integration test for missing prompt file error
description: Add integration test per spec: Missing prompt file results in exit code 1, stderr contains error message. Test both default prompt.md missing and explicit --prompt-file pointing to nonexistent file.
close_reason: Test default_prompt_missing already exists in crates/ralph/tests/integration.rs

title: Update ralph README.md to match spec
description: README.md has multiple issues: (1) References --max-iterations which does not exist, (2) --spec description says it appends ./specs/<stem>.md but code uses fm show (and --spec may be removed per pn-60d64cfc), (3) Missing documentation for --banner, --log-file, --session-id, --resume flags, (4) Examples section incomplete. Update to align with spec CLI Interface section.

title: Implement sgf list built-in command
description: The spec defines 'sgf list' as a reserved built-in that displays available cursus commands with descriptions. It reads .sgf/cursus/ directories (local first, then global), parses the description field from each TOML, and displays a formatted table. Also shows built-in commands. Currently missing from the Commands enum and has no implementation. Files: crates/springfield/src/main.rs.
close_reason: Implemented sgf list built-in: Commands enum variant, run_list output formatting, cursus::list_all() with local/global merge, and 5 unit tests.

title: Remove sgf status stub or document it in spec
description: main.rs has a Commands::Status variant that prints 'not yet implemented'. The spec does not list 'status' as a reserved built-in (only init, list, logs, resume). Either remove this dead stub or add it to the spec if planned. Files: crates/springfield/src/main.rs lines 25-26.

title: Fix monitor_stdin to be unconditional in AFK mode
description: Spec says for AFK mode: 'The controller is created with monitor_stdin: true'. But orchestrate.rs line 262-263 conditionally sets it: monitor_stdin = is_afk && (SGF_MONITOR_STDIN env var set || stdin.is_terminal()). The spec says it should be unconditionally true for AFK mode. The SGF_MONITOR_STDIN env override can remain for testing but the default should match the spec. Files: crates/springfield/src/orchestrate.rs lines 262-263.

title: Integration test for sgf list command
description: Add integration test verifying sgf list displays available cursus commands with descriptions and built-in commands. Test with both local and global cursus directories, verify local overrides global. Files: crates/springfield/tests/integration.rs.

title: Integration test for banner flag passthrough to ralph
description: Add integration test verifying that when a cursus iter has banner=true, ralph receives the --banner flag. Also test that banner defaults to false (no --banner flag). Files: crates/springfield/tests/integration.rs.

title: Update README.md for sgf list command
description: Add sgf list to the Usage section of README.md with example output showing available commands table. Files: README.md.

title: Add E2E test: cycle detection rejects circular dependencies
description: Add integration test: create two issues, add deps forming a cycle (A depends on B, B depends on A), verify the cycle_detected error is returned. Specified in the spec Testing section.

title: Add E2E test: single-fix auto-close
description: Add integration test: create a bug, create one fix task with --fixes, close the fix task, verify the bug is auto-closed with reason 'fixed'. Specified in spec Testing section as distinct from the multi-fix scenario.

title: Add E2E test: ready includes unplanned bugs
description: Add integration test: create a bug with no fix tasks, verify it appears in pn ready output. Specified in spec Testing section.

title: Add E2E test: ready excludes planned bugs
description: Add integration test: create a bug, create a fix task with --fixes, verify the bug no longer appears in pn ready (since it has open/in-progress fix tasks). Specified in spec Testing section.

title: Add E2E test: doctor --fix releases in_progress claims
description: Add integration test: create issues, set them to in_progress, run pn doctor --fix, verify all in_progress items are released back to open status. Specified in spec Testing section.

title: Add E2E test: JSON output shape validation
description: Add integration test: verify --json output from key pn commands matches the shapes documented in the spec JSON Output section. Verify issue objects contain all required fields (id, title, status, issue_type, priority, spec, created_at, updated_at, description, assignee).

title: Add CLI-level stem validation for forma commands
description: The spec Error Handling section requires: 'Invalid stem (spaces, uppercase) -> Reject at CLI level with validation error.' Currently stems are passed through to the daemon without CLI-side validation. Add validation in main.rs to reject stems containing spaces, uppercase chars, or other invalid characters before making HTTP requests.

title: Fix result event styling to use default instead of bold white
description: The spec says result event text should be printed with 'default styling', but run_afk() at main.rs lines 741-747 applies bold white (same as agent text). Change result text rendering to use default/unstyled output.

title: prompt_file_flag_passed_as_append_system_prompt test fails: gets file content instead of study directive
close_reason: Fixed test assertions to match spec: --prompt-file content is inlined, not passed as study directives. Also fixed multi-line arg capture parsing.

title: parse_global_cursus_tomls and parse_global_spec_cursus_multi_iter tests fail due to stale expected description strings
description: Tests in crates/springfield/src/cursus/toml.rs compare against hardcoded description strings that no longer match the actual .sgf/cursus/ TOML files. parse_global_cursus_tomls expects 'Implementation loop' but gets 'Claim and implement one issue from the backlog'.
close_reason: Updated test expectations to match current global cursus TOML files
